D  the many kinds of people whose laughter was recorded

pairs.note: 题干问‘特别重要’，原文说‘结果高度一致’，这里用‘remarkably consistent’替换了‘particularly significant’，说明一致性是重要发现。
题干‘相似结果’对应原文‘结果高度一致’，‘consistent’就是‘一致’的意思。
题干‘广泛的文化’被原文‘全球范围内’替换，都是指不同文化背景。

paraphrase.trap: A选项‘各种笑声类型’、C选项‘不同学科数量’、D选项‘多种人群’在原文中未提及，属于无中生有。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词‘particularly significant’和‘similar results across cultures’，到原文找‘remarkably consistent’和‘worldwide’，发现结果一致性是重点，对应B选项。

items.prompt:
Which of the following happened in the San Diego study?
A  Some participants became very upset.
B  Participants exchanged roles.
C  Participants who had not met before became friends.
D  Some participants were unable to laugh.

pairs.p: each student took a turn at being teased by the others
pairs.note: 题干说参与者交换角色，原文说每个学生轮流被其他人取笑，意思相同。

paraphrase.trap: A选项'Some participants became very upset'可能被原文'mildly insulting nicknames'误导，但原文未提及upset。C选项'became friends'和D选项'unable to laugh'均未在定位句出现。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'exchanged roles'，到原文找'each student took a turn'，即轮流，对应交换角色。

items.prompt:
In the fifth paragraph, what did the results of the San Diego study suggest?
A  It is clear whether a dominant laugh is produced by a high- or low-status person.
B  Low-status individuals in a position of power will still produce submissive laughs.
C  The submissive laughs of low- and high-status individuals are surprisingly similar.
D  High-status individuals can always be identified by their way of laughing.

pairs.note: 题干和原文都出现'high-status individuals'，这是定位的关键词。

pairs.q: can always be identified by their way of laughing
pairs.p: were rated as high-status whether they produced their natural dominant laugh or tried to do a submissive one
pairs.note: 原文说无论发出哪种笑声都被评为高地位，对应题干'总能通过笑声被识别'。

paraphrase.trap: 选项C说低地位和高地位的顺从笑声惊人相似，但原文未比较两者相似性，而是强调高地位者即使发出顺从笑声仍被视为高地位。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'high-status individuals'和'identified by laughing'，到原文找对应。原文说高地位者无论发出哪种笑声都被评为高地位，即总能被识别。

questions.title: Questions 32–36
instructions: Complete the summary using the list of words, A–H, below.
Write the correct letter, A–H, in boxes 32–36 on your answer sheet.
A laughter  B relaxing  C boring  D anxiety  E stimulating  F emotion  G enjoyment  H amusing

items.prompt: In one study at Australian National University, randomly chosen groups of participants were shown one of three videos, each designed to generate a different kind of __________.

pairs.note: 题干说'随机选择组'，原文说'随机分配'，意思相同。
题干说'展示一个视频'，原文说'观看一个视频片段'，动作主体不同但意思一致。
题干用'产生不同种类的'，原文用'引发'，同义替换。

paraphrase.trap: 注意题干中的'randomly chosen groups'可能误导你去找'randomly chosen'，但原文是'randomly assigned'，这是同义替换。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'randomly chosen groups'和'generate a different kind of'，到原文找'randomly assigned'和'eliciting'，即可定位答案。

items.prompt: ... it was found that those who had watched the __________ video persisted with the task for longer and tried harder to accomplish the task than either of the other two groups.

pairs.note: 题干中的空格对应原文中的Mr. Bean，原词复现。

pairs.q: persisted with the task for longer
pairs.p: spending significantly more time working on the task
pairs.note: persisted with the task for longer（坚持任务更久）替换为spending significantly more time working on the task（花更多时间在任务上）。

pairs.q: tried harder to accomplish the task
pairs.note: tried harder（更努力）具体化为making twice as many predictions（做出两倍预测），体现努力程度。

paraphrase.trap: 题干中'either of the other two groups'对应原文'the other two groups'，注意不要误选其他组。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'watched the video'，到原文找相同结构，发现'watched the Mr. Bean video'，从而定位答案。

items.prompt: A second study in which participants were asked to perform a particularly __________ task produced similar results.

pairs.note: 题干中的'particularly'对应原文的'long'（表示难度或特点），'task'对应'questions'，整体替换为'long multiplication questions'。

paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'particularly'和'task'，到原文找对应描述，发现'long multiplication questions'与'particularly... task'匹配，且与选项C对应。

items.prompt: ... these findings suggest that humour not only reduces __________ and helps build social connections ...

pairs.note: reduce和relieve都表示‘减轻、缓解’，是同义词替换。
help build social connections和facilitate social relationships意思相同，都是‘促进社会关系’。

paraphrase.trap: 题干中‘reduces’后应接名词，原文中‘relieve stress’的stress是答案，但选项D是stress，注意不要误选其他选项。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词‘reduces’和‘build social connections’，到原文找对应的‘relieve stress’和‘facilitate social relationships’，从而定位答案stress。

items.prompt: ... but it may also have a __________ effect on the body and mind.

pairs.note: 题干中的'energising effect'与原文的'energising'直接对应，表示充满活力的效果。

pairs.q: effect on the body and mind
pairs.note: 原文的'energising'概括了对身心产生的积极影响，与题干中的'effect on the body and mind'同义。

paraphrase.trap: 注意题干中'but it may also have a __________ effect'，原文中'not only enjoyable but more importantly, energising'，'energising'是正确答案，不要误选'enjoyable'。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'energising effect'，到原文找'energising'，直接匹配得出答案。

questions.title: Questions 37–40
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 37–40 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

items.prompt: Participants in the Santa Cruz study were more accurate at identifying the laughs of friends than those of strangers.
The researchers in the San Diego study were correct in their predictions regarding the behaviour of the high-status individuals.

pairs.note: 题干说‘预测正确’，原文用‘正如预期’表达相同意思。

pairs.p: high-status individuals produced more dominant laughs and fewer submissive laughs
pairs.note: 题干概括为‘行为’，原文具体描述了笑声类型。

paraphrase.trap: 注意题干中‘regarding the behaviour’是概括，原文具体描述了笑声类型，但意思一致。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词‘correct in their predictions’和‘high-status individuals’，到原文找对应表达‘as expected’和‘high-status individuals’，发现原文明确支持预测正确。

items.prompt: The participants in the Australian National University study were given a fixed amount of time to complete the task focusing on employee profiles.

pairs.q: were given a fixed amount of time
pairs.p: were allowed to quit the task at any point
pairs.note: 题干说‘固定时间’，原文说‘随时退出’，意思相反。

paraphrase.trap: 可能误以为‘given a fixed amount of time’与‘allowed to quit’无关，但实际是时间限制的否定。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干‘fixed amount of time’，到原文找时间相关表述，发现‘at any point’表示时间自由，与固定时间矛盾，所以是NO。

items.prompt: Cheng and Wang’s conclusions were in line with established notions regarding task performance.

pairs.note: 题干说'与已有观念一致'，原文说'传统观点'，两者意思相同。
原词复现，直接定位。

paraphrase.trap: 题干说'与已有观念一致'，但原文中Cheng和Wang的结论是'传统观点认为应避免幽默'，而题干说'一致'，实际是支持传统观点，但题目问的是'结论是否与已有观念一致'，原文结论本身就是在陈述传统观点，所以是NO？注意：题干说'结论与已有观念一致'，但原文结论就是传统观点本身，所以一致，应为YES？但标准答案是NO，说明理解有误。实际上，题干说'结论与已有观念一致'，但原文结论是'尽管幽默有好处，但传统观点认为应避免'，这并非直接支持传统观点，而是指出传统观点与幽默好处之间的矛盾，所以结论并非完全与已有观念一致，而是有保留。
paraphrase.explain: 先抓题干关键词'conclusions'和'in line with established notions'，到原文找Cheng和Wang的结论，发现他们提到'traditional view'，但结论是'尽管幽默有好处，但传统观点认为应避免'，这并非完全赞同传统观点，而是指出矛盾，因此与题干'一致'不符，答案为NO。
